title: Naming Things
description: Learn to store and work with text (strings) in your programs.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Variables can hold more than just numbers. They can also hold text!

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: In programming, we call text a 'string'. It's like a string of characters tied together!

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Exactly! But strings need to be wrapped in quotes. Try typing: crop_type = "wheat"

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Perfect! You created a string variable that stores the word 'wheat'!

phases.title: Quotes Matter
phases.description: Learn about single and double quotes

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: You can use double quotes (") or single quotes ('). Both work the same!

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Let's create another string. Type: farmer_name = 'Qubit'

postDialogues.name: Qubit
postDialogues.content: So I used single quotes this time. Both work?

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Yes! Just make sure you start and end with the same type of quote!

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Here's something cool - you can join strings together using the + sign!

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: This is called 'concatenation'. Try typing: message = "Growing " + crop_type

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Now 'message' contains "Growing wheat"! The two strings are joined together!

postDialogues.name: Qubit
postDialogues.content: I noticed you added a space after 'Growing'. Is that important?

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Good catch! Without the space, it would be "Growingwheat" - no space between!

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Let's print our message to see it! Type: print(message)

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Great! When you run this, you'll see "Growing wheat" in the output!

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Click the play button to see your strings in action!

objectives.description: Click play to run your code

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Excellent! You now know how to work with text in your programs!

postDialogues.name: Qubit
postDialogues.content: I can store crop names, messages, anything that's text!

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Exactly! Next, let's learn about a special type of variable - booleans!
